title: append-block-item has no dry-run; only the script validates the prospective whole file
description: file-block-item.ts builds the prospective whole block (on-disk items + new item) and validates it (dry-run PASS == write PASS). The append-block-item op has no dryRun param (the upsert-block-item op does, ops-registry.ts:553), so the CLI cannot offer append dry-run. Closable CLI-side without an op change via client-side prospective-validation.

evidence.reference: script builds + validates the prospective whole file for dry-run
upsert-block-item op declares dryRun; append-block-item op does not — the asymmetry
GAP-6 in the parity survey; recommended CLI-only-shared-helper (no op change)

impact: A CLI user cannot preview an append without writing, unlike the script and unlike upsert — an ergonomic and safety gap on the highest-traffic write path.
proposed_resolution: Add a cli.ts pre-call branch for append-block-item --dry-run that runs the lifted file-block-item.ts:247-255 validation (read block + build prospective + validateFromFile) and returns WITHOUT invoking appendToBlock. No op change (avoids adding dryRun to the frozen op). Re-confirm via the canonical pipeline.
